subject: Gaming
title: Counter-Strike: The FPS That Defined Esports
description: A short lesson to help you apply Counter-Strike: The FPS That Defined Esports.

hook.text: Counter-Strike is esports' longest-running shooter. Over 25 years later, millions still compete daily.

buildup.text: The no-respawn format meant every death mattered. Tension built naturally round after round.

discovery.text: Team coordination mattered more than aim. The best teams communicated like military units.

twist.text: CS:GO skin gambling created a black market worth hundreds of millions before regulators intervened.

climax.text: CS Majors now fill 20,000-seat arenas. A fan mod became one of the biggest esports on the planet.

punchline.text: A fan mod became a 25-year esports dynasty. Community built greatness.

quiz.question: What made Counter-Strike's competitive format so tense?
options: No respawns meant every death mattered in each round
Players could only use one weapon
